name: harvester
version: v6 — multi-texture pipeline: stone foundation rim, metal axle, yellow-tinted metal hub, separate wood texture for braces vs posts/platform/blades, two-stage AO bake to exclude rotor shadow on platform
purpose: Erstes und meistgebautes Gebäude — extrahiert Energie aus dem Boden des Hex. Sitzt auf einer base_plate. Animierter Rotor (rotiert um Z-Achse — horizontale Wheel-Plane).

HV_FoundationRim.shape: Top rim, r=0.70, h=0.025 — wider than post-corner distance 0.636 so posts sit firmly
HV_FoundationRim.material: mx_foundation_stone (castle_wall_slates × neutral tint) — visible grey stone band where posts sit

HV_Posts.shape: 4 inward-tapered square wooden beams (base 0.45, top 0.20 from center, height 1.30)

HV_Braces.shape: 3 levels of X-pattern wooden cross-braces (24 diagonal struts total)
HV_Braces.material: mx_wood_braces (worn_planks × warm tint) — DIFFERENT wood texture than posts to add visual variety

HV_AxleSupport.shape: Small steel cube 0.10×0.10×0.14 sitting on the platform
HV_AxleSupport.material: mx_axle_metal (rusty_metal_05 × cool steel tint) — proper metal texture, not flat palette

HV_Rotor.shape: Horizontal turbine rotor: vertical steel axle + small yellow hub + 8 long propeller-style wooden blades + small yellow dome cap
HV_Rotor.blade_orientation: horizontal plane (wheel face = up); blades are radial
HV_Rotor.pivot: Origin set to (0, 0, 2.33) — top of axle support box
HV_Rotor.anim_empty: anim_spin_z (PLAIN_AXES at pivot) — Three.js rotates HV_Rotor around Z-axis
HV_Rotor.hub_material: mx_harvester_yellow (rusty_metal_05 × strong yellow tint — painted-metal look)

polyhaven_textures_used: wood_planks_dirt (512px diffuse) — posts, platform, blades (different tints)
worn_planks (512px diffuse) — X-braces (different wood texture for visual variety)
castle_wall_slates (512px diffuse) — foundation rim stone band
rusty_metal_05 (512px diffuse) — foundation pad, axle, axle support, yellow hub (different tints)

material_tint_family.mx_harvester_yellow: rusty_metal_05 × (1.60, 1.20, 0.25) — strong yellow tint = painted metal hub
material_tint_family.mx_wood_braces: worn_planks × (1.05, 0.90, 0.70) — different wood texture for X-braces

material_pipeline: Image Texture → Tint MixRGB(MULTIPLY) → VColor MixRGB(MULTIPLY) → BSDF Base Color. All slots use real PolyHaven diffuse textures × per-material tint × VColor(AO bake). NO flat palette colors anywhere.
render_pipeline: Flat-shading + Cycles AO bake → vertex color Col → Texture × VColor MULTIPLY in materials. MeshBasicMaterial-tauglich für Three.js.

ao_bake_strategy.static_pass: Rotor moved to z=-100, hide_render=True, all Cycles ray visibility flags set to False during bake of static parts (FoundationPad, FoundationRim, Posts, Braces, Platform, AxleSupport)
ao_bake_strategy.rotor_pass: Rotor baked in isolation at original position so it has its own internal AO without contributing shadows to static parts
ao_bake_strategy.rationale: Rotor will spin in-game — any baked rotor shadow on the platform would look wrong as the rotor rotates around it
ao_bake_strategy.verification: Test render with rotor rotated 22.5° shows blade shadows (live Cycles shadows) move with rotor; baked AO on platform top stays uniform = 1.0

techniques_applied: Diffuse-only (no PBR maps — no normal/roughness/AO textures included)
Posts (4) + Braces (24) + Rotor (12) joined to single meshes
Texture sharing: rusty_metal_05 used for 3 different materials (axle, axle support, yellow hub) via tint variations; wood_planks_dirt for 3 wood materials

threejs_runtime_hints.rotor_animation: scene.getObjectByName('HV_Rotor').rotation.z = t * 0.8  // ~0.8 rad/s spin around vertical axis
threejs_runtime_hints.anim_empty: anim_spin_z empty marks the pivot. HV_Rotor's origin already set to it, so direct rotation.z works.
threejs_runtime_hints.shading_note: Use MeshBasicMaterial — model is fully flat-shaded with AO baked into vertex color attribute 'Col'. No live lighting/shadows needed.

design_lineage.v4: 12-blade wagon-wheel rotor on tall steel mast, vertical wheel plane, mixed wood/steel tower
design_lineage.v5: 8 long propeller blades on horizontal plane, all-wood tower with palette-colored axle support — re-built with Tripo3D AI v2 generation as visual reference
design_lineage.v6: Multi-texture pipeline (no palette colors anywhere): stone foundation rim, separate wood texture for braces, real metal texture for axle/yellow hub, two-stage AO bake to keep platform top clean for the rotating rotor
